options.text: RBI chose to keep its benchmark policy rate unchanged.
It retained its accommodative stance to withhold inflation and to provide an environment conducive to growth.
RBI asked the government to buy dollars, partly to prevent the dollars from flooding the market and thus depressing the value of dollar

questions.question_text: RBI chose to keep its benchmark policy rate unchanged because:

options.text: The pandemic bombarded the country with many challenges such as a substantial period of economical shutdown.
The recent hardening of crude price, owing to the geopolitical crisis, has heightened the uncertainty surrounding the inflation outlook.
The sterilization in repo rate will impact the housing activity because most loanees rely on floating rates and will face increased equated installments
Inflation is a function of rising expectations and going by that, there is some amount of inflationary steam left in the economy, that repo rate is unable to target.

questions.question_text: Why is the RBI's discretion of retaining the repo rate not enough to curtail the inflation?

options.text: RBI started to target the segment of population where financial inclusion is still not being penetrated enough.
RBI's effort to put bar on unregulated moneylender thus making these indirect weapons of money control fall under the ambit of RBI's regulation
By introducing SDF at 3.75%, RBI approaches towards liquidity management and normalization of LAF corridor

questions.question_text: How does the RBI manage to work with monetary policy corridor without tempering its policy rates?

options.text: As RBI revised upwards its CPI inflation forecast to 5.7 per cent for the FY23, as compared to the fallacious 4.5 per cent projected earlier.
Claiming inflation nothing but transitory, RBI was being widely criticized for not unwinding its monetary stimulus,
The sudden deficit in borrowing rate of 10 years government bond which was preached to be a public good that had to be kept low.

questions.question_text: Why is the RBI perceived to be behind the curve on inflation control?

options.text: The Minister has presented a high fiscal deficit substantiating that it is for strengthening investment, which in turn can "crowd-in" private investment.
She justified fiscal deficit as a growth enhancing tool in the times of pandemic, provided it adheres to numeric threshold of deficit.
She stated the quality of fiscal consolidation suffers if the fiscal deficit is just an expenditure compression rather than increased tax buoyance
The finance minister stated that the outlay for capital expenditure in overall fiscal deficit calculation is being stepped up sharply by 35.4%.

questions.question_text: What was finance minister’s remark on high fiscal deficit?

options.text: While the household inflation is expected to rise at 11%, the average deposit rate across all banks stands at 5%.
With high inflation rate and slow growth, the possibility of attaining 7.2% growth rate in this fiscal is in a doubtful state.
India stands out as a high wealth disparity country with top 15% holding maximum wealth and bottom 40% are struggling.

Find the cost price of the article S, if marked price of the article is Rs.350?
If the cost price of article P is 20% more than cost price of article Q, then find the marked price of article P is what percentage of marked price of article Q?
If the selling price of article T is Rs.238, then find the difference between marked price and cost price of article T?
On article R, if there is an additional discount of 10% is offered, the profit earned by him would be Rs.42, find the cost price of article R?
If marked price of article P and S is same, then selling price of article P is what percentage more or less than selling price of article S?
Vessel contains 40 liters wine. If x liters of wine is replaced with water, this process is repeated two more times and then quantity of wine in the vessel is 20.48 liters, find the value of x?
Perimeter of square is 48 cm and length of a rectangle is equal to side of square and breadth of rectangle is 4 cm less than length of the rectangle. Find the area of rectangle.

The average age of A, B & C is 48 years. Ratio of present ages of A to C is 3:5 and present age of B is 33.33% more than that of A. Find the difference between age of A after 5 years to age of B two years ago.

P, Q & R started a business with investment of Rs.28000, Rs.16000 & Rs.20000 respectively. After the 6 months P added 25% more of his initial investment and Q withdrew Rs.6000. At the end of the year profit earned by R is Rs.3120, then find the difference between profit share of P and of Q.

Speed of boat in still water is 60% more than speed of current and total time takes by boat to cover certain distance going downstream and upstream is 32 hours. Find the time taken by boat to cove same distance in still water.

Three pipe P1, P2 and P3 fill the tank in 15 hours, 10 hours and 20 hours respectively. If all the three pipes opened together and pipe P2 & P1 are closed after three hours, then pipe P3 fill the remaining tank. Find the total time to fill whole tank.

Joshi invest Rs.4000 at the rate of R% simple interest for four years and he got Rs.1600 as interest. If he invests same amount for 2 years in C.I. and rate of interest increased by 4%. Find the interest received by joshi after 2 years.

If the ratio of marked price and selling price of an article is 13:10. Shopkeeper earned 25% profit. If the difference between marked price and cost price of an article is Rs.450. find the selling price of an article.
Train crosses a bridge which is 250 m long in 25 sec. Find the time taken by train to cross a man running in opposite direction at speed of 15m/sec. Find the speed of train.
